title: cuscuta polyanthemos
intro: Cuscuta polyanthemos, also known as Dodder of Hawthorn or Wood Dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta polyanthemos include its ability to improve circulation, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and neurodegenerative diseases. Cuscuta polyanthemos is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
